title: Resonance spectroscopy of 44Ti in the excitation energy range 9.05 to 10.28 MeV

x4freetext: Resonance spectroscopy of 44Ti in the excitation
energy range 9.05 to 10.28 MeV

x4codes.hlp: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC), Trombay, Mumbai, India

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Zeitschrift fuer Physik A, Hadrons and Nuclei, Vol.317, p.209 (1984)

x4codes.hlp: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC), Trombay, Mumbai, India

x4freetext: Four surface barrier detectors were used to
detect the scattered particles at the laboratory angles
Targets were prepared by the vacuum evaporation of
natural CaF2 (96.92% 40Ca) on thin carbon film backings
(~8 ug/cm2). The total target thickness used was
about 5 keV at 4 MeV alpha particle energy
The total error on the average comprising
of errors in:
* Statistical error (~3%)
Uncertainty due to the normalising constants;
Uncertainty due to the target nonuniformity
The data was analysed in terms of the R matrix theory.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.
ANALYSIS -> ADD-RES.
